ques: In the Culture section, identify the latest film release reviewed and provide a brief summary of the review.
answer: The latest film reviewed in BBC Culture's Film & TV section is *Disclosure Day*, Steven Spielberg's new alien drama, which received a 2-star (★★☆☆☆) review. The review calls it Spielberg's most disappointing film — a "flimsy," outdated car-chase thriller likened to "a drab X-Files episode" that recycles old ideas (a Men in Black-style cover-up plot) and falls far short of Close Encounters and ET. Josh O'Connor stars as a cyber-security worker leaking alien secrets; Emily Blunt's psychic weather-forecaster is the highlight, while Colin Firth is panned as a badly miscast, generic villain.
